name: telecode-ai
description: The first remote-controlled autonomous coding agent in your IDE.

commands.title: TeleCode AI: Set Response Style - Short
TeleCode AI: Set Response Style - Normal
TeleCode AI: Set Response Style - Detailed

telecode.model.description: Model ID (e.g., arcee-ai/trinity-large-preview:free). You can type any custom model ID manually in settings.json

telecode.apiKey.description: API key for the selected provider

telecode.baseUrl.description: Custom base URL for OpenAI-compatible APIs (e.g., http://localhost:11434/v1)

telecode.telegram.apiRoot.description: Telegram Bot API root URL (advanced networking)

telecode.telegram.forceIPv4.description: Force IPv4 for Telegram API requests (helps on broken IPv6 networks)

telecode.whatsapp.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable the agent to be controlled remotely via WhatsApp Web. After enabling, start the agent and scan the QR code in the **Chat logs** using **Linked Devices** on your phone.

telecode.whatsapp.sessionPath.markdownDescription: Local directory where the WhatsApp Web authentication session is stored. Allows reconnecting across VS Code restarts without rescanning. Default: `~/.telecode-ai/`

telecode.whatsapp.allowSelfCommands.markdownDescription: Allow the primary linked number to send commands (`/run`, `/stop`, etc.) to itself (useful as a personal remote notepad).

markdownEnumDescriptions: **Safest**. The agent only ignores everyone except you.
Only numbers specified in `Allowed Phones` can command the agent.
**DANGEROUS**. Any number that texts you can control the agent.
telecode.whatsapp.accessMode.markdownDescription: Control who is allowed to send task commands via WhatsApp.

telecode.whatsapp.allowedPhones.markdownDescription: Comma-separated list of phone numbers (e.g. `+12025550111, +447700900000`) allowed to use the agent. Only applies if `accessMode` is set to `allowlist`.

telecode.logMaxChars.description: Maximum in-memory UI log buffer size before trimming old lines

telecode.channelLogLines.description: Maximum number of per-channel log lines kept in memory

enumDescriptions: Only key status phases and tool boundaries
Balanced status output for daily work
telecode.statusVerbosity.description: How many runtime status lines are shown in Logs

enumDescriptions: Read/search focused. No bash and no out-of-workspace access
Daily coding profile with normal tools, workspace-only scope
Maximum capability mode. Full tools and out-of-workspace enabled

telecode.dev.autoReloadWindow.description: Development only: reload Extension Host window automatically when dist/extension.js changes

enumDescriptions: Concise answers - only the most important parts
Normal answers - balance between details and brevity

telecode.allowOutOfWorkspace.description: Allow agent to access files and run commands outside the current workspace folder

scripts.build:all: npm run build:extension && npm run build:webview
scripts.vsix:build: mkdir -p builds && npx vsce package --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository -o builds/
scripts.vsix:rebuild:same: npm run build:all && npm run vsix:build
scripts.vsix:rebuild:patch: npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && npm run vsix:rebuild:same
scripts.release:patch: mkdir -p builds && vsce package patch --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository -o builds/
scripts.release:minor: mkdir -p builds && vsce package minor --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository -o builds/
scripts.release:major: mkdir -p builds && vsce package major --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository -o builds/
